ai_strategy_lead.name: Tech AI / AI Makerspace
ai_strategy_lead.title: Public AI strategy or initiative lead / hub identified from cited sources

ai_strategy.summary: Georgia Institute of Technology has a public AI posture anchored in Tech AI / AI Makerspace. The cited sources show activity across responsible-use guidance, teaching resources, research or infrastructure, and AI literacy or workforce preparation.

key_provisions: Use AI transparently and follow course, research, workplace and academic-integrity expectations.
Do not enter confidential, regulated, sensitive, unpublished or protected institutional data into unapproved public AI tools.
Human users remain accountable for accuracy, bias, copyright, privacy and final decisions based on AI output.
ai_policy.summary: Georgia Institute of Technology publishes AI policy or guidance with a comprehensive stance: AI use is allowed or actively explored, but bounded by privacy, ethics, academic integrity, compliance and human-review expectations.

bodies.name: Tech AI / AI Makerspace
bodies.scope: Public AI strategy, guidance, teaching, research or technology coordination.

governance_structure.summary: AI governance at Georgia Institute of Technology is evidenced through Tech AI / AI Makerspace and the cited policy or strategy sources. Public sources did not identify a separate chief AI officer during this sweep.

initiatives.title: AI Makerspace course redesign and supercomputing classroom
initiatives.description: Georgia Tech's College of Engineering describes the AI Makerspace as a digital sandbox for students and instructors to use NVIDIA-accelerated AI supercomputing in coursework, including redesigned AI-for-engineering courses and a dedicated educational AI supercomputer.
initiatives.significance: The initiative ties generative AI infrastructure directly to course redesign rather than treating AI compute as research-only capacity.

investment.funding_source: Georgia Tech, NVIDIA, and Penguin Solutions partnership; dollar amount not publicly disclosed
investment.description: Public sources disclose the AI Makerspace hardware configuration but not a total contract value or institutional spend.

collaborations.description: Georgia Tech partnered with NVIDIA and Penguin Solutions to provide AI supercomputing for engineering education.

tools_and_vendors.name: NVIDIA HGX H100 and HGX H200 systems
tools_and_vendors.role: GPU-accelerated AI compute for student coursework and faculty course redesign

initiatives.policy_implications: The makerspace shifts AI assessment and pedagogy toward supervised, institutionally supported AI use rather than unsupported external tools.

ai_academic_programs.name: AI teaching, training, degree, certificate or literacy offering

computing_infrastructure.summary: Georgia Tech's AI Makerspace is a dedicated educational AI supercomputer. Public sources describe 20 NVIDIA HGX H100 systems and 18 NVIDIA HGX H200 systems; assuming 8 GPUs per HGX system, the disclosed configuration totals 304 GPUs.

narrative_summary: Georgia Institute of Technology has a documented AI posture across policy, teaching, research, infrastructure or access. The central public signal is Tech AI / AI Makerspace, supported by policy or guidance sources that encourage responsible use while emphasizing privacy, academic integrity, ethics and human oversight. The institution also shows curriculum or AI literacy activity and at least one research, center, infrastructure or applied innovation signal. Its maturity is strongest where public sources show durable structures: committees, centers, portals, degree programs, supercomputing, enterprise tools, or workforce initiatives. The gaps are also important: this sweep did not find a standalone chief AI officer in public sources for most assigned institutions, and several initiatives do not disclose exact budgets, user counts or implementation metrics. Overall, the evidence supports a medium-confidence R1 profile with enough public signals to compare the institution across governance, teaching, research, enterprise tools, workforce and equity dimensions.

gaps_acknowledged: Public sources did not identify a standalone chief AI officer role during this sweep.
The AI Makerspace hardware capacity is public, but Georgia Tech did not disclose a total dollar amount, contract value, or exact active user count for the makerspace in the reviewed sources.
Leadership names were not populated because the assigned task prioritized missing AI posture profiles and every non-obvious AI claim is URL-cited.
